title: Need to set date format and spanish in forms
excerpt: I use Monday in Spanish and tried some forms but date format cannot be set to DD/MM/YY and does not apear in spanish on the form

posts.author: Michel (Michel Manghi)
posts.cookedHtml: I use Monday in Spanish and tried some forms but date format cannot be set to DD/MM/YY and does not apear in spanish on the form

posts.author: Izobelle (Isabelle)
posts.cookedHtml:
Same here. Even if I set in he settings ‘‘dd.mm.yyyy’’ it stills shows ‘‘mm.dd.yyy’’ wen selecting a timeline in forms ! It’s confusing for Europeans.

Thanks!

posts.author: Izobelle (Isabelle)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi do you have noted this feature?

posts.author: Nick94 (Nicola)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi, this is quiet frustrating.

I have experienced a similar issue when using the quotes and invoices view.
I can input the date as dd/mm/yyyy (as an Italian, this is what I want), but when exporting to the final document the date is rearranged in mm/dd/yyyy.

Has anyone experienced the same?
